title: Crypsinus trisectus
taxonomy: Crypsinus trisectus is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to Asia and is a type of climbing fern. The classification of Crypsinus trisectus is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Polypodiaceae, Genus: Crypsinus, Species: C. trisectus. This species is known for its distinctive trisect leaves and is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Crypsinus trisectus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other ferns in the Polypodiaceae family.

intro: Crypsinus trisectus, also known as Three-leafed Crypt, is a type of fern commonly found in East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and alleviate symptoms of fever and cough.
